GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "Indigenous Australian fashion designer Lyn-Al Young has created a limited-edition deodorant for NIVEA inspired by her silk designs", "For those familiar with Lyn-Al Young's designs under her namesake label, Lyn-Al, it will come as no surprise that she views her stunning silk pieces as wearable art. "I work mostly with beautiful, soft silk to create one-off pieces," she tells Vogue of her design process. "Each one tells a deep story connected to me, my family, my mob and Country." Through her designs, she celebrates her Gunnai, Wiradjuri, Gunditjmara and Yorta Yorta culture and so, naturally, that's how she approached this project too. "I'm an Indigenous Australian fashion designer. My grandmother is a Yorta Yorta woman and grew up by the Murray River," Young says. "It's a culturally significant place for me and I feel more connected when I paint there." As part of this collaboration with Vogue and NIVEA, Young chose to hero this deep connection to Country and explained to Vogue how working in places of ancestral significance gives power to her designs. "While the silk I work with is so delicate, going out in the harsh elements to paint on Country grounds me and gives me so much strength. This combination allows me to share a story in a soft-spoken way that carries a lot of power." It's a juxtaposition that can be witnessed in her chosen design for NIVEA Black & White Silky Smooth Deodorant too. She refers to her design by name as Burrai Wula, which means 'baby emu', a motif that has a special meaning to her too. "I chose this because emus are a symbol of guidance on the path to find wisdom and truth," she explains. "I love how strong and graphic the image is, and it holds a lot of meaning for me." For this Melbourne-based designer, working with NIVEA was a "special experience" that also aligned with how she values her designs and the wearability of beautiful clothes. "Because all of my pieces are originals and carry so much meaning, I love that NIVEA Black & White Silky Smooth deodorant protects clothing and the wearer too." NIVEA Black & White Silky Smooth deodorant is formulated with special conditioners to keep skin soft and it also protects your precious pieces—be them wearable art or your favourite black T-shirt—from marks. "It's soft on skin and doesn't leave marks on delicate fabrics like my silk and wraps," Young agrees. "I paint with peace, love and joy and I hope women feel that when they wear my designs. I want them to feel beautiful, protected and empowered to be exactly who they are." You can shop the limited-edition deodorant here."
